name: Chilled Red Pepper Soup
recipeIngredient: 42 oz Roasted red peppers (water-packed), rinsed and blotted dry, with all black bits removed
4 cups Frozen or fresh Corn kernels
8 oz Low- or non-fat yogurt (for garnish)
3 large Flour tortillas (cut into 12 sections each)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor fitted with a steel chopping blade, combine the roasted red peppers and garlic. Puree until smooth. Scrape the mixture into a large non-aluminum bowl.
2. Add the chicken stock, buttermilk, sugar, salt, corn, pickled nopales, oregano, and Tabasco sauce to the bowl. Stir to blend thoroughly.
3. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
4. In a small bowl, combine the yogurt and minced fresh oregano. Cover and refrigerate.
5. To make the crisps: Preheat the broiler. Place tortilla wedges on a lightly oiled cookie sheet. Brush with oil, sprinkle with oregano (crumbled) and Parmesan cheese.
6. Under the broiler, crisp and lightly color the tortilla wedges, watching closely to prevent burning. Before serving, ladle soup into bowls.
7. Add about a tablespoon of yogurt in the center. Sprinkle chopped oregano over the soup and serve with the crisped tortilla wedges.
Note: If pickled nopales are unavailable, use chopped pickled jalapeno peppers (seeds removed) or omit them entirely.
